title: The Immune System
puzzle: How does your body recognize what belongs and what might be dangerous?
insight: The immune system is a layered defense network of cells, signals, and organs that constantly distinguishes between familiar tissue and possible threats.
key_ideas: Some defenses respond quickly and broadly.
Others learn specific threats and remember them.
Balance matters, because too much reaction can also cause harm.
mind_blower: Your body runs an endless identification challenge without asking for your attention.
next_curiosity: If immune memory matters so much, how do vaccines take advantage of it?
narration_text: How does your body recognize what belongs and what might be dangerous? The immune system is a layered defense network of cells, signals, and organs that constantly distinguishes between familiar tissue and possible threats. Some defenses respond quickly and broadly. Others learn specific threats and remember them. Balance matters, because too much reaction can also cause harm. Your body runs an endless identification challenge without asking for your attention. If immune memory matters so much, how do vaccines take advantage of it?
